generatedSentence: Amidst the `hammam`’s `volumetric` projections, a `veejay` found `hearing` a cryptic `haiku` the `topic` of a tense `gabfest`, knowing their `partner`, an `affluent` `hardliner`, `foresaw` the `downside` to their `employment` under an `intractable` `regime` that would `upbraid` any `principle` `offered` `athwart` `prosperity`, keeping `inventories` of `rubbers` and `offcuts` `crammed` in a discreet `envelope`. Suddenly, a `ghastly` `hurricane` raged `afield`, bringing `rain` that created a `melting` `whirlpool` effect, while a `missile` exploded with a `hic`, having `trampled` the `grassy` `biome`, and a `ruffian` in `uniform` and `leggings` appeared, `hackling` a `lookout` while attempting to `manhandle` a `goblet`, `therefore`, their `kinfolk` were `ushered` into a `hushed` `oriental` enclave where `organic` `jackfruits` were `stacked`. A `hammy` `dumbass`, with a `tumor` throbbing in his `armpit`, `inhaled` deeply, his `eardrum` surprisingly `aligned` to the distorted `newsreel` of `hadiths` that `gave` him `vertigo`; he `worked` `uphill`, past `landmines` and `rusted` `catapults`, where he `poached` a `peach` and `pickled` `jawbones`, observing `kakapos` with a `frisky` `whiffle` of feathers, confronting this `block` in the `logjam` that `inflicted` a `trivial` `ending` to his `heritage`’s `dimension`. He `evaluated` the `artic` `vowels` of the ancient `glyph` on his `trousers`, knowing he must `cast` away `nothingness`, `licking` at his final `calorie` as a `mantis` `gunship` of `vanadium` alloy `aligned` and `hitchhiked` through the air, its `faculty` `opposite` his own, the only resolution `offered` by the `sunscreen` of time.

A `hammy` `dumbass`, with a `tumor` throbbing in his `armpit`, `inhaled` deeply, his `eardrum` surprisingly `aligned` to the distorted `newsreel` of `hadiths` that `gave` him `vertigo`; he `worked` `uphill`, past `landmines` and `rusted` `catapults`, where he `poached` a `peach` and `pickled` `jawbones`, observing `kakapos` with a `frisky` `whiffle` of feathers, confronting this `block` in the `logjam` that `inflicted` a `trivial` `ending` to his `heritage`’s `dimension`.

Suddenly, a `ghastly` `hurricane` raged `afield`, bringing `rain` that created a `melting` `whirlpool` effect, while a `missile` exploded with a `hic`, having `trampled` the `grassy` `biome`, and a `ruffian` in `uniform` and `leggings` appeared, `hackling` a `lookout` while attempting to `manhandle` a `goblet`, `therefore`, their `kinfolk` were `ushered` into a `hushed` `oriental` enclave where `organic` `jackfruits` were `stacked`.

A `hammy` `dumbass`, with a `tumor` throbbing in his `armpit`, `inhaled` deeply, his `eardrum` surprisingly `aligned` to the distorted `newsreel` of `hadiths` that `gave` him `vertigo`; he `worked` `uphill`, past `landmines` and `rusted` `catapults`, where he `poached` a `peach` and `pickled` `jawbones`, observing `kakapos` with a `frisky` `whiffle` of feathers, confronting this `block` in the `logjam` that `inflicted` a `trivial` `ending` to his `heritage`’s `dimension`.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone explorer figure wearing a tattered trench coat and a glowing visor. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A lone explorer figure sits in a futuristic hammam, listening intently to a volumetric projection, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A ghastly hurricane with a missile impact rips through a grassy biome, a ruffian in uniform struggles with a goblet, mid-shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The explorer figure works uphill, past rusted catapults, observing frisky kakapos, low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The explorer figure looks at an ancient glyph on their trousers as a vanadium gunship approaches in the artic sky, dramatic close-up.

definitions.definition: (stative) To perceive sounds through the ear.
definitions.example: I was deaf, and now I can hear.

definitions.definition: (stative) To perceive (a sound, or something producing a sound) with the ear, to recognize (something) in an auditory way.
definitions.example: I heard a sound from outside the window.

definitions.definition: To exercise this faculty intentionally; to listen to.
To listen favourably to; to grant (a request etc.).
definitions.example: Eventually the king chose to hear her entreaties.

definitions.definition: To receive information about; to come to learn of.
(with from) To be contacted by.
To listen to (a person, case) in a court of law; to try.
definitions.example: Your case will be heard at the end of the month.

definitions.definition: To sympathize with; to understand the feelings or opinion of.
definitions.example: You're tired of all the ads on TV? I hear ya.

definitions.definition: The sense used to perceive sound.
definitions.example: My hearing isn't what it used to be, but I still heard that noise.

definitions.definition: The act by which something is heard.
A proceeding at which discussions are heard.
definitions.example: There will be a public hearing to discuss the new traffic light.

definitions.definition: A legal procedure done before a judge, without a jury, as with an evidentiary hearing.
A scolding.
Able to hear.
definitions.example: Deaf people often must deal with hearing people.

definitions.definition: A communal bathhouse in Islamic countries.
A Japanese poem in three lines, the first and last consisting of five morae, and the second consisting of seven morae, usually with an emphasis on the season or a naturalistic theme.
A three-line poem in any language, with five syllables in the first and last lines and seven syllables in the second, usually with an emphasis on the season or a naturalistic theme.
definitions.example: Haiku, a poem

definitions.definition: A severe tropical cyclone in the North Atlantic Ocean, Caribbean Sea, Gulf of Mexico, or in the eastern North Pacific off the west coast of Mexico, with winds of 119 km/h (74 miles per hour) or greater accompanied by rain, lightning, and thunder that sometimes moves into temperate latitudes.
A wind scale for quite strong wind, stronger than a storm
(nutrition) Kilogram calorie or large calorie. A unit of energy 1000 times larger than the gram calorie. It is equivalent to the gram kilocalorie, approximately 4.2 kilojoules.

The gram calorie or small calorie, a non-SI unit of energy, equivalent to approximately 4.2 joules. This unit was widely used in chemistry and physics, being the amount of energy needed to raise the temperature of 1 gram of water by 1 °C.
Someone who presents a television programme of videos; a video jockey.
To work as a video jockey; to present videos.
Pertaining to measurement by volume.
Any object used as a weapon by being thrown or fired through the air, such as stone, arrow or bullet.
definitions.example: The Rhodians, who used leaden bullets, were able to project their missiles twice as far as the Persian slingers, who used large stones.

definitions.definition: A self-propelled projectile whose trajectory can be adjusted after it is launched.
definitions.example: That missile is explosive enough to kill hundreds.

definitions.definition: To change (or to be changed) from a solid state to a liquid state, usually by a gradual heat.
definitions.example: I melted butter to make a cake.

definitions.definition: To dissolve, disperse, vanish.
definitions.example: His troubles melted away.

definitions.definition: To soften, as by a warming or kindly influence; to relax; to render gentle or susceptible to mild influences; sometimes, in a bad sense, to take away the firmness of; to weaken.
To be discouraged.
To be emotionally softened or touched.
definitions.example: She melted when she saw the romantic message in the Valentine's Day card.

definitions.definition: To be very hot and sweat profusely.
definitions.example: I need shade! I'm melting!

definitions.definition: The process of changing the state of a substance from solid to liquid by heating it past its melting point.
The act of softening or mitigating.
Which is melting, dissolving or liquefying.
Given over to strong emotion; tender; aroused; emotional, tearful.
That causes one to melt with emotion; able to make others feel tender and emotional.
A figure carved in relief or incised, especially representing a sound, word, or idea.
Any non-verbal symbol that imparts information.
A visual representation of a letter, character, or symbol, in a specific font and style.
A vertical groove.
An occasion (such as a meeting) where people talk at length.
The act of reproaching; scorn; disdain.
To criticize severely.
(followed by with or for, and formerly of before the object) To charge with something wrong or disgraceful; to reproach
To treat with contempt.
(followed by "to" before the object) To object or urge as a matter of reproach
To utter upbraidings.
To vomit; retch.
A vantage point with a view of the surrounding area.
A session of watching for an approaching enemy, police, etc.
definitions.example: We kept a lookout all night, but nobody came.

definitions.definition: A person on watch for approaching enemy, police, danger, etc.
definitions.example: The raid failed when the lookout noticed the enemy group.

definitions.definition: A subject for observation; a prospect or view.
One's perspective, outlook; hence, one's responsibility. (used with a possessive pronoun or a noun in a possessive form).
definitions.example: Every man's interest is his own lookout.

definitions.definition: Subject; theme; a category or general area of interest.
definitions.example: A society where a topic cannot be discussed, does not have free speech.

definitions.definition: Discussion thread.
A musical sign intended to suggest a particular style or genre.
An argument or reason.
An external local application or remedy, such as a plaster, a blister, etc.
Topical
Mode of rule or management.
definitions.example: a prison regime

definitions.definition: A form of government, or the government in power.
definitions.example: a capitalist regime

definitions.definition: A period of rule.
A regulated system; a regimen.
definitions.example: Heaven will eliminate the tyrannical regimes.

definitions.definition: A division of a Mafia crime family, led by a caporegime.
A set of characteristics.
definitions.example: A typical annual water level regime would include a gradual summer drawdown beginning in early May.

definitions.definition: Pliable material derived from the sap of the rubber tree; a hydrocarbon polymer of isoprene.
Synthetic materials with the same properties as natural rubber.
An eraser.
A condom.
Someone or something which rubs.
The cushion of an electric machine.
The rectangular pad on the pitcher's mound from which the pitcher must pitch.
definitions.example: Jones toes the rubber and then fires to the plate.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) Water-resistant shoe covers, galoshes, overshoes.
definitions.example: Johnny, don't forget your rubbers today.

definitions.definition: Tires, particularly racing tires.
definitions.example: Jones enters the pits to get new rubber.

definitions.definition: A hardship or misfortune.
In relation to a series of games or matches between two competitors where the overall winner of the series is the competitor which wins a majority of the individual games or matches:
A rubber match; a game or match played to break a tie.
The game of rubber bridge.
Rubber overshoes; galoshes.
A disease of sheep, with great heat and itchiness.
The work or occupation for which one is used, and often paid
The act of employing
A use, purpose
definitions.example: The personnel director handled the whole employment procedure

definitions.definition: The state of being employed
An activity to which one devotes time
The number or percentage of people at work
A paper or cardboard wrapper used to enclose small, flat items, especially letters, for mailing.
Something that envelops; a wrapping.
A bag containing the lifting gas of a balloon or airship; fabric that encloses the gas-bags of an airship.
A mathematical curve, surface, or higher-dimensional object that is the tangent to a given family of lines, curves, surfaces, or higher-dimensional objects.
A curve that bounds another curve or set of curves, as the modulation envelope of an amplitude-modulated carrier wave in electronics.
The shape of a sound, which may be controlled by a synthesizer or sampler.
The information used for routing a message that is transmitted with the message but not part of its contents.
An enclosing structure or cover, such as a membrane; a space between two membranes
The set of limitations within which a technological system can perform safely and effectively.
The nebulous covering of the head or nucleus of a comet; a coma.
An earthwork in the form of a single parapet or a small rampart, sometimes raised in the ditch and sometimes beyond it.
Not tractable; not able to be managed, controlled, governed or directed.
(of a mathematical problem) Not able to be solved.
(of a problem) Difficult to deal with, solve, or manage.
(of a person) Stubborn; obstinate.
Difficult to treat (of a medical condition).
(operations) The stock of an item on hand at a particular location or business.
definitions.example: Due to an undersized inventory at the Boston outlet, customers had to travel to Providence to find the item.

definitions.definition: (operations) A detailed list of all of the items on hand.
definitions.example: The inventory included several items that one wouldn't normally think to find at a cheese shop.

definitions.definition: (operations) The process of producing or updating such a list.
definitions.example: This month's inventory took nearly three days.

definitions.definition: A space containing the items available to a character for immediate use.
definitions.example: You can't get through the underground tunnel if there are more than three items in your inventory.

definitions.definition: (operations) To take stock of the resources or items on hand; to produce an inventory.
definitions.example: The main job of the night shift was to inventory the store, and restock when necessary.

definitions.definition: A piece that has been cut off of a larger piece when not needed; surplus.
To propose or express one's willingness (to do something).
definitions.example: She offered to help with her homework.

definitions.definition: To present in words; to proffer; to make a proposal of; to suggest.
definitions.example: Everybody offered an opinion.

definitions.definition: To place at someone’s disposal; to present (something) to be either accepted or turned down.
definitions.example: He offered use of his car for the week.  He offered his good will for the Councilman's vote.

definitions.definition: To present (something) to God or gods as a gesture of worship, or for a sacrifice.
To place (something) in a position where it can be added to an existing mechanical assembly.
To bid, as a price, reward, or wages.
definitions.example: I offered twenty dollars for it. The company is offering a salary of £30,000 a year.

definitions.definition: To happen, to present itself.
To make an attempt; typically used with at.
To put in opposition to; to manifest in an offensive way; to threaten.
definitions.example: to offer violence to somebody

definitions.definition: To dress (flax or hemp) with a hackle; to prepare fibres of flax or hemp for spinning.
To separate, as the coarse part of flax or hemp from the fine, by drawing it through the teeth of a hackle or hatchel.
To tear asunder; to break into pieces.
Someone who is associated with another in a common activity or interest.
One of the pieces of wood comprising the framework which strengthens the deck of a wooden ship around the holes through which the mast and other fittings pass.
A group financial arrangement in which each member contributes a set amount of money over a set period.
To join as a partner.
(often with with) To work or perform as a partner.
The condition of being prosperous, of having good fortune
A fundamental assumption or guiding belief.
definitions.example: We need some sort of principles to reason from.

definitions.definition: A rule used to choose among solutions to a problem.
definitions.example: The principle of least privilege holds that a process should only receive the permissions it needs.

definitions.definition: (sometimes pluralized) Moral rule or aspect.
definitions.example: I don't doubt your principles.

definitions.definition: A rule or law of nature, or the basic idea on how the laws of nature are applied.
definitions.example: Bernoulli's Principle

definitions.definition: A fundamental essence, particularly one producing a given quality.
definitions.example: Many believe that life is the result of some vital principle.

definitions.definition: A beginning.
A source, or origin; that from which anything proceeds; fundamental substance or energy; primordial substance; ultimate element, or cause.
An original faculty or endowment.
To equip with principles; to establish, or fix, in certain principles; to impress with any tenet or rule of conduct.
A swirling body of water.
definitions.example: A whirlpool is an instance of a vortex produced by ocean tides, or by a hole underneath where the water would drain out, such as in a bathtub.

definitions.definition: A hot tub, jacuzzi.
Turmoil, or agitated excitement.
To spin or swirl like the water in a whirlpool.
Somebody who is wealthy.
A stream or river flowing into a larger river or into a lake; a tributary stream; a tributary.
Abundant; copious; plenteous.
(by extension) Abounding in goods or riches; having a moderate level of material wealth.
definitions.example: They were affluent, but aspired to true wealth.

definitions.definition: Tributary.
Flowing to; flowing abundantly.
From side to side; across.
definitions.example: Above, the stars appeared to move slowly athwart.

definitions.definition: Across the path (of something).
definitions.example: a fleet standing athwart our course

definitions.definition: Wrongly; perplexingly.
From one side to the other side of.
definitions.example: The stars moved slowly athwart the sky.

definitions.definition: Across the line of a ship's course or across its deck.
definitions.example: The damaged mainmast fell athwart the deck, destroying the ship's boat.

definitions.definition: Across the path or course of; opposing.
Away (from the home or starting point, physical or conceptual); usually preceded by far (or farther, further).
On the field.
definitions.example: We now have both teams afield and can begin the match.

definitions.definition: Out in the open.
To move something heavy by force of men, without aid of levers, pulleys, machine, or tackles.
To assault or beat up a person.
To mishandle; to handle roughly; to mangle.
To control (a machine, vehicle, situation, etc.) by means of physical strength.
A disadvantageous aspect of something that is normally advantageous.
definitions.example: The downside of obtaining a higher rank is that far more work is expected.

definitions.definition: A downward tendency, especially in the price of shares etc.
A single aspect of a given thing.
A measure of spatial extent in a particular direction, such as height, width or breadth, or depth.
A construct whereby objects or individuals can be distinguished.
The number of independent coordinates needed to specify uniquely the location of a point in a space; also, any of such independent coordinates.
The number of elements of any basis of a vector space.
One of the physical properties that are regarded as fundamental measures of a physical quantity, such as mass, length and time.
definitions.example: The dimension of velocity is length divided by time.

definitions.definition: Any of the independent ranges of indices in a multidimensional array.
A universe or plane of existence.
To mark, cut or shape something to specified dimensions.
To award something by means of a raffle or random drawing, often used with off.
definitions.example: They raffled off four gift baskets.

definitions.definition: To participate in a raffle.
definitions.example: to raffle for a watch

definitions.definition: Having the edge finely notched.
A scoundrel, rascal, or unprincipled, deceitful, brutal and unreliable person.
A pimp; a pander.
A lover; a paramour.
To play the ruffian; to rage; to raise tumult.
Brutal; cruel; savagely boisterous; murderous.
definitions.example: ruffian rage

definitions.definition: An inhalation.
To draw air into the lungs, through the nose or mouth by action of the diaphragm.

To draw air or any form of gas (either in a pure form, or mixed with small particles in form of aerosols/smoke -sometimes stemming from a medicament) into the lungs, through the nose or mouth by action of the diaphragm.
To eat very quickly.
Something opposite or contrary to something else.
An opponent.
An antonym.
definitions.example: "Up" is the opposite of "down".

definitions.definition: An additive inverse.
Located directly across from something else, or from each other.
definitions.example: She saw him walking on the opposite side of the road.

definitions.definition: Of leaves and flowers, positioned directly across from each other on a stem.
Facing in the other direction.
definitions.example: They were moving in opposite directions.

definitions.definition: Of either of two complementary or mutually exclusive things.
definitions.example: He is attracted to the opposite sex.

definitions.definition: Extremely different; inconsistent; contrary; repugnant; antagonistic.
In an opposite position.
definitions.example: I was on my seat and she stood opposite.

definitions.definition: Facing, or across from.
definitions.example: He lives opposite the pub.

definitions.definition: In a complementary role to.
definitions.example: He played opposite Marilyn Monroe.

definitions.definition: On another channel at the same time.
definitions.example: The game show Just Men! aired opposite The Young and the Restless on CBS.

definitions.definition: Resembling or characteristic of ham.
Amateurish; characterized by overacting.
One of the great tendons situated in each side of the ham, or space back of the knee, and connected with the muscles of the back of the thigh.
The biceps femoris, semimembranosus, and semitendinosus muscles.
An approximation to the sound of a hiccup, used e.g. to indicate drunkenness.

definitions.definition: A person who takes a firm, uncompromising position on some policy.
Any of various of rice-based dishes common in Louisiana Cajun or Creole cooking; most often with shrimp, oysters, chicken or ham.
To cook something in simmering liquid.
To be cooked in simmering liquid
To become soft or muddy.

To begin and not complete.

Cooked by poaching.
Obtained by poaching.
A tree (Prunus persica), native to China and now widely cultivated throughout temperate regions, having pink flowers and edible fruit.
The soft juicy stone fruit of the peach tree, having yellow flesh, downy, red-tinted yellow skin, and a deeply sculptured pit or stone containing a single seed.
A light moderate to strong yellowish pink to light orange color.
A particularly admirable or pleasing person or thing.
Of or pertaining to the color peach.
Particularly pleasing or agreeable.
To preserve food (or sometimes other things) in a salt, sugar or vinegar solution.

Preserved by pickling.

definitions.definition: To effect by gradual degrees.

definitions.definition: To embroider with thread.
To set into action.

definitions.definition: To cause to ferment.
To ferment.
To exhaust, by working.

definitions.definition: To shape, form, or improve a material.

definitions.definition: To operate in a certain place, area, or speciality.

definitions.definition: To operate in or through; as, to work the phones.
To provoke or excite; to influence.

definitions.definition: To cause to happen or to occur as a consequence.

definitions.definition: To cause to work.

definitions.definition: To function correctly; to act as intended; to achieve the goal designed for.

definitions.definition: To influence.

definitions.definition: To effect by gradual degrees; as, to work into the earth.

definitions.definition: To behave in a certain way when handled

Wrought.

A pattern that "fires" out other patterns.

An expert.

definitions.definition: To speed something up.

definitions.definition: To offer vigorous support to a person or cause.

definitions.definition: To seek to attack someone; to take aim at someone.

An uphill route.

Going up a slope or a hill.

definitions.definition: An article of clothing that covers the part of the body between the waist and the ankles or knees, and is divided into a separate part for each leg.

definitions.definition: To press, force, or drive, particularly in filling, or in thrusting one thing into another; to stuff; to fill to superfluity.

definitions.definition: To put hastily through an extensive course of memorizing or study, as in preparation for an examination.

To arrange in a stack, or to add to an existing stack.

definitions.definition: To take all the money another player currently has on the table.

definitions.definition: To deliberately distort the composition of (an assembly, committee, etc.).

definitions.definition: To crash; to fall.

definitions.definition: To operate cumulatively.

definitions.definition: Unfairly constructed, as a stacked deck of cards.
